2026年のWBC（ワールドベースボールクラシック）に出場する侍ジャパンのメンバーは、1月16日と1月26日の2段階で発表されました[2][1]。

## 投手

**メジャーリーガー**
- 大谷翔平（ドジャース）[1]
- 菊池雄星（エンゼルス）[1]
- 松井裕樹（パドレス）[1]
- 菅野智之（FA）[2]

- 伊藤大海（日本ハム）[2]
- 平良海馬（西武）[2]
- 種市篤暉（ロッテ）[2]

- 曽谷龍平（オリックス）[1]
- 北山亘基（日本ハム）[1]
- 松本裕樹（ソフトバンク）[2]

## 捕手

- 若月健矢（オリックス）[2]
- 坂本誠志郎（阪神）[2]
- 中村悠平（ヤクルト）[1]

## 内野手

- 源田壮亮（西武）[2]
- 牧秀悟（DeNA）[2]
- 牧原大成（ソフトバンク）[2]

- 村上宗隆（ホワイトソックス）[1]
- 小園海斗（広島）[1]

## 外野手

- 近藤健介（ソフトバンク）[2]
- 周東佑京（ソフトバンク）[2]
- 森下翔太（阪神）[2]
- 鈴木誠也（カブス）[1]

メジャーリーガーは史上最多の8人が選出されており[1]、前回大会（2023年）の優勝メンバーから9選手が選ばれています[4]。

2026年WBC（ワールド・ベースボールクラシック）の侍ジャパン（日本代表）メンバーは、2026年1月時点で**投手11名、捕手3名、内野手8名、外野手4名の計26名が発表済みで、残り1枠が後日決定予定**です[1][2][3]。以下にポジション別に整理します（一部ソースで表記揺れあり、最新発表を優先）。

### **投手（11名）**
- 大谷翔平（ドジャース、投打二刀流）[1][3][5]
- 菊池雄星（エンゼルス）[1][3][5]
- 松井裕樹（パドレス）[1][3]
- 松本裕樹（ソフトバンク）[2][3]
- 伊藤大海（日本ハム）[1][3]
- 平良海馬（西武）[1][3][5]
- 菅野智之（オリオールズ）[2][3]
- 種市篤暉（ロッテ）[3][5]
- 大勢（巨人）[3][5]
- 高橋宏斗（中日）[2][7]
- 石井大智（楽天）[2][5]

### **捕手（3名）**
- 若月健矢（オリックス）[2][3][4]
- 坂本誠志郎（阪神）[2][3][4]
- 中村悠平（ヤクルト）[2][4]

### **内野手（8名）**
- 源田壮亮（西武）[1][3]
- 牧原大成（ソフトバンク）[1][2][3]
- 牧秀悟（DeNA）[2][3]
- 佐藤輝明（阪神）[1][2][3]
- 村上宗隆（ホワイトソックス、新規）[1][2]
- 小園海斗（広島、新規）[1][2]
- 岡本和真（ブルージェイズ、新規）[1][2]
- 周東佑京（ソフトバンク、代走・内野）[1][2][3]

### **外野手（4名）**
- 近藤健介（ソフトバンク）[1][2][3]
- 森下翔太（阪神）[1][2][3]
- 鈴木誠也（カブス、新規）[1][2]

**追加情報**: 1月16日に11名、1月26日に10名発表（山本由伸ら一部期待選手は不参加）[1][2]。監督は井端弘和[6]。最終ロースターは大会直前に確定予定[1][4]。

1770126905319.topic: 2026 WBC侍ジャパン出場メンバー完全確定
1770126905319.objective: 最終ロースター確定、残り捕手枠・外野代打枠の発表、佐々木朗希・今永昇太などの追加選出確認
1770126905319.last_search_result:
2026年WBC（ワールド・ベースボール・クラシック）の侍ジャパン（日本代表）のメンバーは、段階的に発表されており、2026年1月26日時点で少なくとも29人（投手15人、野手14人）が**出場予定選手として決定**していますが、完全確定ではなく一部枠（捕手残り1枠、外野代打枠など）が残っています[1][2][3][5][7]。

### 発表状況の詳細
- **12月26日発表（第一陣、投手中心8人）**: 松井裕樹（パドレス）、伊藤大海（日本ハム）、大勢（巨人）、大谷翔平（ドジャース）、菊池雄星（エンゼルス）、種市篤暉（ロッテ）、平良海馬（西武）、石井大智（阪神）[1][4][7]。
- **1月16日発表（11人）**: 牧秀悟（DeNA）、若月健矢（オリックス）、牧原大成（ソフトバンク）、源田壮亮（西武）、佐藤輝明（阪神）、近藤健介（ソフトバンク）、坂本誠志郎（阪神）、菅野智之（FA）、周東佑京（ソフトバンク）、森下翔太（阪神）、松本裕樹（ソフトバンク）[1][3][7]。
- **1月26日発表（10人）**: 宮城大弥（オリックス）、山本由伸（ドジャース）、髙橋宏斗（中日）、曙谷龍平（オリックス）、北山亘基（日本ハム）、中村悠平（ヤクルト）、岡本和真（ブルージェイズ）、村上宗隆（ホワイトソックス）、鈴木誠也（カブス）、小園海斗（広島）[2][3][5]。
- **主なポジション構成（29人ベース）**:
  | ポジション | 主な選手 |
  |-------------|----------|
  | 投手 (15人) | 大谷翔平、山本由伸、松井裕樹、宮城大弥など[2][5] |
  | 捕手 | 若月健矢、坂本誠志郎、中村悠平（残り1枠）[1][5] |
  | 内野 | 村上宗隆、岡本和真、佐藤輝明、源田壮亮、小園海斗など[3][5] |
  | 外野 | 近藤健介、森下翔太、鈴木誠也、周東佑京など（代打枠未定）[3][5] |

一部ソースで佐々木朗希や今永昇太の名前が挙がっていますが、公式発表リストには未確認で、MLB球団調整待ちの可能性があります[4][6]。3月開幕に向け、追加発表が予定されています[1][6]。

### Sources:

- https://www.youtube.com/shorts/gj4qte7vHaE
- https://spocale.com/blog/wbc_member/

1770127416824.last_search_result:
**Artificial intelligence (AI)** is the capability of computer systems or machines to perform tasks that typically require human intelligence, such as learning, reasoning, problem-solving, perception, decision-making, language understanding, and pattern recognition.[1][2][5][9]

This field of computer science develops software and methods enabling machines to perceive environments, learn from data, and take actions to achieve goals, often without explicit programming.[1][6][7] AI simulates human cognitive processes like comprehension and creativity, powering applications in self-driving cars, medical diagnosis, natural language processing, and recommendation systems.[2][3][4]

### Key Subfields and Concepts
- **Machine Learning (ML)**: Algorithms learn from data to make predictions or decisions, forming a core layer under AI.[2][4]
- **Deep Learning**: Uses multi-layered neural networks for complex pattern recognition in images, text, and video, enabling large-scale automation.[2][3]
- **Narrow AI (ANI)**: Current systems excelling at specific tasks, like speech recognition.[7][10]
- **General AI (AGI or Strong AI)**: Theoretical systems matching human-level intelligence across diverse tasks; not yet achieved and requiring major advances.[2][5][7][10]

AI draws from disciplines like data science, neuroscience, and linguistics, transforming industries by automating tasks, reducing errors, and analyzing vast datasets with speed and accuracy.[6][8]

**Sources:**
- [1] https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence
- [2] https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/artificial-intelligence
- [3] https://www.darktrace.com/cyber-ai-glossary/artificial-intelligence
- [4] https://www.nasa.gov/what-is-artificial-intelligence/
- [5] https://www.coursera.org/articles/what-is-artificial-intelligence
- [6] https://www.mtu.edu/computing/ai/
- [7] https://www.iso.org/artificial-intelligence/what-is-ai

- [9] https://www.merriam-webster.com/dictionary/artificial%20intelligence
- [10] https://learning.nd.edu/resource-library/ai-overview-and-definitions/

1770293197874.objective: Track Starship V3 maiden flight readiness, launch date confirmation, and vehicle preparation milestones
1770293197874.last_search_result:
The next **Starship launch** (Flight 12, the maiden flight of the **Starship V3** variant) is targeting **March 2026** from Starbase, Texas, using Booster 19 and Ship 39 as the first V3 full stack.[1][3][6][7]

### Key Preparation Details
- SpaceX completed five Starship flights in 2025, with Flight 12 now focusing on V3 vehicles under production in the Starfactory; a potential February-March window was discussed early in 2026, but schedules have slipped to March NET (no earlier than).[3][6]
- Booster 19 (Super Heavy) and Ship 39 (upper stage, first V3 ship) are in final preparations, including composite overwrap pressure vessels (COPVs).[3][6]
- No firm date is set, listed as TBD March 2026 across trackers, with live coverage expected via sites like RocketLaunch.Live.[1][7]

### Recent Context and Broader SpaceX Schedule
- Past launches: As of early January 2026, Starship had multiple test flights in 2023-2025, with outcomes ranging from failures (e.g., Flight 1 in April 2023) to partial successes (e.g., Flight 3 in March 2024).[4]
- Near-term SpaceX activity is dominated by **Falcon 9** launches, including Starlink missions on February 14 and 16, 2026, and others in early February; Starship remains the focus for major tests.[1][5]
- Longer-term: SpaceX plans ~5 Starship upper stages to Mars in Q4 2026 transfer window; no NASA Starship-specific events listed before March.[2][4]

Schedules are dynamic and subject to delays for testing, weather, or regulatory approval.

### Sources:
- [1] https://rocketlaunch.org/launch-schedule/spacex
- [2] https://www.nasa.gov/events/
- [3] https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=VPFsL1Cw92U
- [4] https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Starship_launches
- [5] https://www.spacex.com/launches
- [6] https://nextspaceflight.com/starship/
- [7] https://www.rocketlaunch.live/?filter=starship-bfr
- [8] https://www.visitspacecoast.com/launches

**Sources:**

1770293197874.suggested_prompt: Regarding SpaceX Starship Flight 12's preparation during February-March 2026, please continuously monitor the maiden flight status of Starship V3 vehicles, including Booster 19 and Ship 39 assembly and testing progress, official launch date announcements from SpaceX or Elon Musk, FCC licensing updates, pad readiness at Starbase's Orbital Launch Pad 2, Raptor V3 engine performance data, and any delays or technical anomalies. 1770293322416.objective: Monitor Flight 12, V3 testing, FAA approvals, infrastructure
1770293322416.last_search_result:
**Recent SpaceX Starship updates include FAA approval for up to 44 launches and 88 landings per year at Kennedy Space Center's LC-39A, ongoing preparation for Starship Flight 12 with Version 3 prototypes Ship 39 and Booster 19, and active testing of Block 3 vehicles at Starbase.**[2][5][4]

### FAA Approval at Kennedy Space Center
The US Federal Aviation Administration issued a Record of Decision on January 30, 2026, approving Starship-Super Heavy operations at LC-39A, enabling 44 launches annually (44 Starship upper stage landings and 44 Super Heavy booster landings).[2]

### Starship Flight 12 and Version 3 Preparations
- Ship 39, the first **Version 3 Starship upper-stage prototype**, and Booster 19 are slated for **Flight 12**, marking the debut of the v3 full stack.[5][1]
- Testing at Starbase's Massey Outpost includes an eighth round of cryogenic testing on Ship 39.1 Block 3 test tank, with structural testing campaigns concluding and new hardware arriving for infrastructure like the Air Separation Plant.[4]
- Booster hardware is rolling to the launch site, hinting at a potential debut aligned with a June 2026 timeline for ship-to-ship propellant transfer, alongside plans to expand to five active launch pads.[1]
- Recent activities include removing the blue compression cap and ship aft simulator ring from a tank, with its status (usable or scrapped) unclear.[1]

### Broader Development Context
SpaceX anticipates **Version 4** as a stretched design following Version 3 success, with next major milestones including long-duration flight tests and in-space propellant transfer for the Human Landing System (HLS).[1][3]
Previous flights provide background: Flight 11 used Ship 38 (final v2 Ship) and Booster 17 (final v2 Booster, unflown); Flight 10 used Booster 16 and Ship 37.[5]

**Sources:**
- [1] https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=5N6Aau2sjQ4
- [2] https://gulfnews.com/world/americas/fully-reusable-rockets-starship-gets-green-light-for-44-launches-88-landings-per-year-1.500431064
- [3] https://www.spacex.com/updates
- [4] https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JNqwV4XQqzg
- [5] https://nextspaceflight.com/starship/

sample_apple_intelligence_jp.topic: Apple Intelligenceの日本展開と機能アップデート
sample_apple_intelligence_jp.objective: Track Japan release date, features, and device support
sample_apple_intelligence_jp.last_search_result:
# Apple Intelligenceの日本展開と機能アップデート

**Apple Intelligenceは2025年4月にiOS 18.4のアップデートで日本語対応が開始され、現在は日本語ライティングツール、文章校正、要約、トーン変更などの機能が利用可能です。**[1][4]

## 日本語対応の実装

Apple Intelligenceは当初、米国、英国、オーストラリア、カナダなど英語圏のみでの提供でしたが、2025年3月末にAppleが公式に日本語対応を案内しました。[4]日本語対応は、iOS 18.4、iPadOS 18.4、macOS Sequoia 15.4のアップデートの一環として提供されています。[4]

新たにサポートされた言語には、フランス語、ドイツ語、イタリア語、ポルトガル語、スペイン語、日本語、韓国語、簡体字中国語が含まれます。[1]

## 利用可能な機能

日本で利用できるApple Intelligenceの機能は以下の通りです：

- **日本語ライティングツール**[3]
- **日本語の文章校正**[3]
- **要約機能**[3]
- **トーン変更機能**[3]

これらの機能を利用するには、iPhone 16の全モデル、iPhone 15 Pro、iPhone 15 Pro Max、iPad mini（A17 Pro）、M1以降を搭載したiPadとMacのモデルなど、対応するデバイスが必要です。[6]

## 今後のアップデート予定

より高度なSiri機能の強化は2026年に予定されています。[2][5]Appleは当初、iOS 18でApple Intelligenceの進化したSiriをリリースする予定でしたが、より高度なパーソナライズ機能の開発に予想以上の時間がかかるため、2026年への延期を発表しました。[2]

2026年1月12日には、AppleがGoogleの生成AIモデル「Gemini」をApple Foundation Modelsに採用することを発表し、次世代のAI機能強化に向けた動きが進んでいます。[7]

---

updates.update:
Significant new developments include full Japanese functionality rollout in **2026 February via iOS 18.3** (previously planned for 2025 April iOS 18.4 beta/limited),[1][2] with natural language improvements like contextual understanding and keigo support.[1] Additional features now available post-2025: expanded capabilities (e.g., image generation, notification summarization, Safari page summaries, object identification),[4] iOS 26 integration,[5][9] and confirmed broader language support including Japanese in official Apple releases.[5][7] Siri advanced features still delayed to 2026 (no Gemini mention confirmed).[2]

1770391488403.last_search_result:
**React 19.2 is the latest stable release as of early 2026, featuring no breaking changes but including critical security patches for CVE-2025-55182 (React2Shell), new APIs like `<Activity>`, `useEffectEvent`, and `cacheSignal`, plus SSR improvements such as Suspense boundary batching and partial pre-rendering.**[1][4][5]

### Key New Features in React 19.2
- **`<Activity>` component**: Manages visibility and preserves state for UI elements like tabs or modals, enabling prioritization of app "activities" without conditional rendering.[1][4]
- **`useEffectEvent` hook**: Provides stable event handlers in effects, reducing unnecessary dependencies and preparing code for the React Compiler.[1][5]
- **`cacheSignal` API**: Supports React Server Components for better caching.[1]
- **Partial Pre-rendering**: Enables hybrid static/dynamic rendering for improved performance.[1]
- **SSR streaming enhancements**: Suspense boundaries now batch reveals for smoother updates, aligning server and client behavior.[1]
- **Developer tools**: Chrome DevTools Performance Tracks and stricter ESLint rules for more predictable code.[1]

React 19 overall introduces foundational features like the `use()` hook for async promises with Suspense, Server Components/Actions, better Web Components integration, enhanced error messages for hydration mismatches, and concurrent rendering optimizations.[3]

### Related Ecosystem Updates
- **React Native 0.83** (December 2025): Integrates React 19.2 with `<Activity>` and `useEffectEvent`; plans patch to 19.2.1 for security fixes; adds DevTools features and experimental Hermes V1 for JS performance.[4]
- Frameworks like MakerKit SaaS kits run on React 19.2.3 with patches applied.[1]

### Upgrade Recommendations
Update to **React 19.2.3** immediately for security patches; audit effects with `useEffectEvent`, adopt `<Activity>` gradually, and enable strict ESLint rules.[1] Use React Native Upgrade Helper for 0.83.[4] No breaking changes reported across releases.[1][4]

### 2026 Trends and Context
React 19 emphasizes enhanced hooks, React Compiler (used by Instagram), advanced concurrent features like Suspense batching, improved DevTools, and faster toolchains (Vite, Next.js).[2][3] These build toward cleaner async UI, server rendering, and performance.

**Sources:**
- [1] https://makerkit.dev/blog/tutorials/react-19-2
- [2] https://www.mindpathtech.com/blog/reactjs-trends/
- [3] https://www.ksolves.com/blog/reactjs/whats-new-in-react-19

1770393565441.last_search_result:
**React 19's latest stable release is version 19.2.4 (released January 26, 2026), which remains in active support with ongoing features, bug fixes, and security patches.**[3]

### Key Updates in React 19.2
React 19.2 introduces refinements for effects, state management, server-side rendering (SSR), and security, building on React 19's foundation from December 2024. No breaking changes occurred in this minor release, though stricter ESLint rules and security patches (e.g., CVE-2025-55182 for React2Shell) require updates.[1][3]

**New APIs and Features:**
- **`<Activity>` component**: Manages visibility and preserves state for UI sections like tabs or modals, enabling prioritization of app "activities" as an alternative to conditional rendering.[1][5]
- **`useEffectEvent` hook**: Provides stable event handlers in effects, reducing unnecessary dependencies and improving cleanup.[1]
- **`cacheSignal` API**: Supports React Server Components (RSC) for better caching in server environments.[1]
- **Partial Pre-rendering**: Enables hybrid static/dynamic rendering for optimized performance.[1]
- **SSR streaming improvements**: Suspense boundaries now batch reveals during streaming SSR, aligning with client-side behavior for smoother updates and reduced visual jank (e.g., better for animations with `<ViewTransition>`).[1][4]
- **Chrome DevTools Performance Tracks**: Enhanced monitoring tools.[1]
- **RSC stability**: Server Components, directives, and functions are now stable, allowing libraries to target React 19 with `react-server` export conditions.[4]

React Server Components features are production-ready, though underlying bundler APIs may change between 19.x minors.[4]

### Security and Support Status
- Critical patches address vulnerabilities like React2Shell; upgrade to 19.2.3+ or framework-patched versions.[1]
- React 19 receives full support; React 18 gets security fixes only (latest 18.3.1).[3]
- React Native 0.83 (December 2025) integrates React 19.2, adding `<Activity>`, `useEffectEvent`, and DevTools enhancements; plans to patch to 19.2.1.[5]

### Upgrade Recommendations
- Update to 19.2.4; use React 18.3.1 as a stepping stone from older versions due to React 19's breaking changes (e.g., new JSX Transform required, UMD builds removed).[4]
- Audit effects with `useEffectEvent`, enable strict ESLint, and adopt `<Activity>` gradually.[1]
- Frameworks like MakerKit's SaaS kits run on 19.2.3 with patches applied.[1]
- For React Native, use the Upgrade Helper; Expo SDK 55 supports it (January 2026).[5]

Earlier React 19 features (from initial release) include `use()` hook for promises, Server Actions, improved hydration errors, Web Components support, and performance boosts like automatic batching.[2]

**Sources:**
- [1] https://makerkit.dev/blog/tutorials/react-19-2
- [2] https://www.ksolves.com/blog/reactjs/whats-new-in-react-19
- [3] https://endoflife.date/react
- [4] https://github.com/facebook/react/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md
- [5] https://reactnative.dev/blog/2025/12/10/react-native-0.83
- [6] https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=tqjJrXd27m4

1770454484020.last_search_result:
iPhone 17は2025年9月9日（米国時間、現地時間）にAppleから正式発表された標準モデルで、日本時間9月10日（水）に発表され、9月12日（金）午後9時から予約開始、9月19日（金）に日本を含む63以上の国・地域で発売されました[1][2][5][6]。

主な仕様として、**革新的なセンターフレームフロントカメラ**、より大きく明るい**ProMotionディスプレイ**、パフォーマンス向上の**A19チップ**を搭載し、最小ストレージは前世代の2倍の**256GB**（512GBオプションあり）、カラーはブラック、ラベンダー、ミストブルー、セージ、ホワイトの5色です[1]。iOS 26は9月16日（火）に無料アップデートで提供されます[1][3]。

iPhone 17シリーズにはPro/Pro Maxモデルもあり、同様のスケジュールでディープブルー、コズミックオレンジ、シルバーの仕上げが用意されています[3]。なお、iPhone 17eは別途2026年2月頃発表・発売の噂モデルで、標準iPhone 17とは異なります[4][7]。

**購入情報**: Apple Storeやキャリア（例: docomo）で予約可能、在庫状況は発売直後に入荷待ちが発生しやすいです[2][9]。
